key_drivers: Falling real yields (DFII10 1.88%, declining from 2%+) provide broad macro tailwind for risk assets and gold
ECB hawkish pivot (potential 3 hikes in 2026) and RBA rate hike (4.10%) widening rate differentials vs USD, supporting EUR and AUD
Geopolitical risk (US strikes, Iran tensions) elevating safe-haven demand for gold and JPY; elevated VIX at 25 signals risk-off backdrop

fallbacks_used: Copper: Investing.com blocked; used Trading Economics and Bloomberg search results
Gold ETF flows: Used World Gold Council search results (February 2026 data, most recent available)
